invariants: The real-T collar is only for the rationalized degree-16 finite surrogate.
The result does not control the infinite residual Taylor tail.
The result is fixed at k=22 and is not an all-k theorem.
Endpoint PF, RH, Laguerre-Polya membership, and Lambda <= 0 are forbidden as inputs.

matrix_rows.claim: The degree-16 real-T collar scout converts the midpoint coefficient-ratio finite truncation into exact rational polynomial data in u=1/T.

derivative_stencil_rows.interpretation: The weighted-gap log stencil starts at 0 as u->0; positive derivative on the interval implies positivity.
derivative_stencil_rows.name: weighted_gap

product_stencil_rows.interpretation: The companion stencil is positive iff F_k^3*F_(k+2)-F_(k-1)*F_(k+1)^3>0.
product_stencil_rows.name: companion

diagnostics.surrogate_note: Coefficient ratios are rationalized from the degree-16 high-order Taylor midpoint decimals; this is an exact root-count analysis of that finite surrogate, not an Arb coefficient-ball theorem.

matrix_rows.proof_boundary: Exact finite-surrogate setup only; not an Arb coefficient-ball theorem and not an infinite Taylor-tail theorem.

matrix_rows.claim: The four degree-16 surrogate normalizer polynomials F_(k-1), F_k, F_(k+1), F_(k+2) have no roots on 0<u<=1/1156 and are positive there.
matrix_rows.proof_boundary: Finite surrogate real-T certificate only; not a statement about the full zeta tail.

matrix_rows.claim: The B product polynomial F_k^2-F_(k-1)F_(k+1), after removing its u=0 zero, has no roots on 0<u<=1/1156 and is positive there.
matrix_rows.proof_boundary: Finite surrogate real-T B-stencil certificate only; not an all-k theorem.

matrix_rows.claim: The companion product polynomial F_k^3F_(k+2)-F_(k-1)F_(k+1)^3, after removing its u=0 zero, has no roots on 0<u<=1/1156 and is positive there.
matrix_rows.proof_boundary: Finite surrogate real-T companion-stencil certificate only; not an infinite-tail theorem.

matrix_rows.claim: The weighted-gap log-stencil derivative numerator has no roots on 0<u<=1/1156 and is positive there, so the weighted-gap surrogate stencil is positive on the real collar.
matrix_rows.proof_boundary: Finite surrogate derivative certificate only; not a proof of the analytic weighted-gap tail.

matrix_rows.claim: The integer threshold T=1156 from the collar scan is upgraded to a real-T sign collar for the degree-16 rationalized finite surrogate.
matrix_rows.proof_boundary: Finite surrogate upgrade only; not a real-T theorem for the infinite zeta Taylor tail.

matrix_rows.claim: The next proof task is to replace rationalized finite-surrogate signs with interval coefficient control and signed infinite-tail stencil bounds on the same real-T collar.
matrix_rows.proof_boundary: Live theorem-search target only; the interval coefficient and infinite-tail upgrades remain open.

matrix_rows.claim: The rationalized degree-16 finite-surrogate real-T collar proves the direct signed stencil-tail theorem.
matrix_rows.gap: The result uses midpoint coefficient ratios, finite degree 16 truncation, fixed k=22, and no infinite residual Taylor-tail bound.
matrix_rows.proof_boundary: Rejected finite-surrogate promotion only; not scaled-curvature monotonicity, cone entry, RH, or Lambda <= 0.

proof_boundary: Finite theorem-search diagnostic only. It certifies a real-T collar for a rationalized degree-16 finite surrogate at fixed k=22, but it does not prove an Arb coefficient-ball theorem, an infinite Taylor-tail theorem, scaled-curvature monotonicity, cone entry, RH, or Lambda <= 0.

summary.main_finding: For the rationalized degree-16 finite surrogate at k=22, the integer collar threshold T=1156 extends to the whole real half-line T>=1156: the four normalizers are positive, the B and companion product stencils have no stripped roots on 0<u<=1/1156, and the weighted-gap derivative numerator is positive there. This is a finite-surrogate real-T collar, not an infinite Taylor-tail theorem.
